perturbation.original_text: The disclosure of confidential information is for the specific purpose of evaluating technology in the field described above in the first instance, and will normally be between of the University of Bristol and of . Any specific documents or materials which are necessarily prov ided on loan for the above purposes will be specified in a schedule to this Agreement and the receiving party will return these and any other documents or materials subsequently provided to the disclosing party on request.
perturbation.changed_text: The disclosure of confidential data is for the specific purpose of evaluating technology in the field described above in the first instance, and will normally be between of the University of Bristol and of . Any specific documents or materials which are necessarily prov ided on loan for the above purposes will be specified in a schedule to this Agreement and the receiving party will return these and any other documents or materials subsequently provided to the disclosing party on request.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'confidential information' to 'confidential data'. This suggests a narrower scope, implying that only data is protected, not necessarily other forms of confidential information such as know-how or designs. It creates contradiction with the introductory section's definition of 'confidential information', where it also includes know-how and designs in the initial text, which is a contradiction now, with the introduction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For the purposes of this Agreement the term confidential information includes proprietary materials and information relating thereto including without limitation specifications , drawings, designs, computer software and knowhow.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'confidential information' generally. The changed text narrows this to 'confidential data'. The contradicted text explicitly defines 'confidential information' to include a broader range of items such as specifications, drawings, designs, computer software and know-how. By changing 'information' to 'data,' the changed text contradicts the definition provided in the contradicted text, because now only data is protected, and other forms of information (designs, know-how) may not be, creating a clear conflict.

perturbation.original_text: The parties will mark or otherwise designate confident ial information to show expressly or by necessary implication that it is imparted in confidence.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties will mark or otherwise designate confidential assets to show expressly or by necessary implication that it is imparted in confidence.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'confidential information' with 'confidential assets'. It is less clear, creating an inconsistency. This shifts the emphasis from the information itself to potentially tangible assets containing the information, narrowing the scope of what needs to be marked/designated. This contradicts with the intention of protection the information in the introduction part of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For the purposes of this Agreement the term confidential information includes proprietary materials and information relating thereto including without limitation specifications , drawings, designs, computer software and knowhow.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'confidential information', which is broad. The changed text refers to 'confidential assets', which is narrower and more specific. The 'Contradicted Text' defines 'confidential information' to include 'proprietary materials and information relating thereto including without limitation specifications , drawings, designs, computer software and knowhow.' By changing 'information' to 'assets' and shifting the focus to tangible things, this creates a contradiction. The modified text now implies that only tangible assets containing information need to be marked, whereas the contradicted text indicates a broader scope that includes intangible information and know-how.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving party will receive all confidential in formation (whether recorded in writing or by other means or given orally without record) which is discl osed in connection with this Agreement subject to the following conditions: a) it will take all proper and reasonable measures to ensure that the confidentiality of such information is maintained; b) it will not use the information for any commercial purpose or manufacture without obtaining a written licence or other agreement from the disclosing party; c) it will not disclose the information to any third party without written permission; d) it will not disclose the information to employ ees other than those above except to the extent necessary to fulfil the purposes set out above and all such other employees to which it will disclose it will be made aware of the confidential nature of the information, and the conditions of disclosure herein; e) it will not make any copy of or abstract of the information without specific written permission from the disclosing party; f) it will acknowledge the source (i.e. one of the organisations signatory to this Agreement) of, and will mark "Confidential", any drawing, document or software incorporating the information.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party will receive all protected data (whether recorded in writing or by other means or given orally without record) which is discl osed in connection with this Agreement subject to the following conditions: a) it will take all proper and reasonable measures to ensure that the confidentiality of such information is maintained; b) it will not use the information for any commercial purpose or manufacture without obtaining a written licence or other agreement from the disclosing party; c) it will not disclose the information to any third party without written permission; d) it will not disclose the information to employ ees other than those above except to the extent necessary to fulfil the purposes set out above and all such other employees to which it will disclose it will be made aware of the confidential nature of the information, and the conditions of disclosure herein; e) it will not make any copy of or abstract of the information without specific written permission from the disclosing party; f) it will acknowledge the source (i.e. one of the organisations signatory to this Agreement) of, and will mark "Confidential", any drawing, document or software incorporating the information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'confidential information' to 'protected data'. This further narrows the scope. Protected data suggests that only data that is actively protected is covered. This creates a contradiction within the section because the text later refers to 'confidential nature of the information' contradicting the initial change that states is protected data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party will receive all protected data (whether recorded in writing or by other means or given orally without record) which is discl osed in connection with this Agreement subject to the following conditions: a) it will take all proper and reasonable measures to ensure that the confidentiality of such information is maintained; b) it will not use the information for any commercial purpose or manufacture without obtaining a written licence or other agreement from the disclosing party; c) it will not disclose the information to any third party without written permission; d) it will not disclose the information to employ ees other than those above except to the extent necessary to fulfil the purposes set out above and all such other employees to which it will disclose it will be made aware of the confidential nature of the information, and the conditions of disclosure herein; e) it will not make any copy of or abstract of the information without specific written permission from the disclosing party; f) it will acknowledge the source (i.e. one of the organisations signatory to this Agreement) of, and will mark "Confidential", any drawing, document or software incorporating the information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'confidential information' with 'protected data', implying a narrower scope of information covered by the agreement. The explanation specifies that 'protected data' suggests only actively protected data is covered. However, the contradicted text, which is actually the same as the changed text in this instance, refers to 'confidential nature of the information'. This creates a direct contradiction because the initial change restricts the scope to 'protected data' while the clause continues to use 'confidential nature of the information', suggesting a broader set of information is being discussed. A reasonable reader would recognize that 'protected data' and 'confidential information' are not necessarily the same, creating inconsistency and uncertainty about what is covered under the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Under the terms of this Agreement there is no explic it or implied transfer of ownership to the receiving party of any drawings, documents or softwar e, or the copyright subsisting in them.
perturbation.changed_text: Under the terms of this Agreement there is no explicit or implied transfer of ownership to the receiving party of any drawings, documents or software, or the intellectual property subsisting in them.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'copyright subsisting in them' with 'intellectual property subsisting in them'. While copyright is a form of IP, the broader term 'intellectual property' could be interpreted to include patents or trade secrets, which may not be directly transferred but could be indirectly affected by the disclosure, so the text is contradicting in section 3, the terms for copying or abstract information, being that intellectual property is more wide than what is covered there.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party will receive all protected data (whether recorded in writing or by other means or given orally without record) which is discl osed in connection with this Agreement subject to the following conditions: a) it will take all proper and reasonable measures to ensure that the confidentiality of such information is maintained; b) it will not use the information for any commercial purpose or manufacture without obtaining a written licence or other agreement from the disclosing party; c) it will not disclose the information to any third party without written permission; d) it will not disclose the information to employ ees other than those above except to the extent necessary to fulfil the purposes set out above and all such other employees to which it will disclose it will be made aware of the confidential nature of the information, and the conditions of disclosure herein; e) it will not make any copy of or abstract of the information without specific written permission from the disclosing party; f) it will acknowledge the source (i.e. one of the organisations signatory to this Agreement) of, and will mark "Confidential", any drawing, document or software incorporating the information.
perturbation.justification: The original text referred specifically to copyright, while the changed text broadens this to 'intellectual property'. The contradicted text outlines conditions for 'protected data', including restrictions on copying and abstracting information. By expanding the scope to all 'intellectual property', the changed text creates a contradiction because the limitations on copying or abstracting information in section f of the contradicted text may not fully cover the entire scope of 'intellectual property' (e.g., trade secrets), which are now included by the changed term.

perturbation.original_text: Both parties agree that at all times, during and after the current discussions, and thereafter for a period of ten (10) years, starting from the effective date of this Agreement, not to communicate or to divulge to third parties confidential information received from the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Both parties agree that at all times, during and after the current discussions, and thereafter for a period of ten (10) years, starting from the effective date of this Agreement, not to communicate or to divulge to third parties sensitive materials received from the other party.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'confidential information' with 'sensitive materials'. Again, changes the scope. Suggesting only physical items that are sensitive are protected and creating an internal contradiction in the agreement that protects intellectual property too.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Under the terms of this Agreement there is no explicit or implied transfer of ownership to the receiving party of any drawings, documents or software, or the intellectual property subsisting in them.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'confidential information' to 'sensitive materials' narrows the scope of protected information to potentially only physical items. The contradicted text states that intellectual property, such as drawings, documents, or software, are not transferred. By changing the protected information to 'sensitive materials', the amended text creates an in-text contradiction because it no longer explicitly protects the intellectual property mentioned in the contradicted text, making the contract ambiguous and creating a strong contradiction between protecting all information and a subset of 'sensitive materials', based on the perturbation explanation.
